Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]]

Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[Inari (mythology)|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]]

Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:New]]

It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[Inari (mythology)|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]]

revision.comment: 64 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:玉造稲荷神社 up to rev 103869960
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 稲荷神社 |画像 = [[画像:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
拝殿 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[玉造 (大阪市)|玉造]]2丁目3-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 40 |緯度秒 = 40.6 |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 31 |経度秒 = 47.8 |祭神 = [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂大神]]、[[シタテルヒメ|下照姫命]]、[[稚日女尊|稚日女命]]、[[ツクヨミ|月読命]]、[[カグツチ|軻偶突智命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[府社]] |創建 = 伝・[[垂仁天皇]]18年 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = 玉造稲荷神社（通称）
比売社（旧称）
豊津稲荷社（旧称） |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Osaka city }} '''玉造稲荷神社'''（たまつくりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[玉造 (大阪市)|玉造]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[府社]]。[[法人登記|登記]]上の[[宗教法人]]名称は'''稲荷神社'''（いなりじんじゃ）。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば[[垂仁天皇]]18年（[[紀元前12年]]）に[[天皇]]によって創建され、当時は'''[[比売神|比売社]]'''と称していた。[[蘇我氏]]と[[物部氏]]の戦いの際、蘇我氏方の[[聖徳太子]]がこの地、玉作岡に布陣して戦勝を祈願し、戦勝後当地に[[観音堂]]を建てたという伝承がある。 豊臣[[大坂城]]の三の丸に位置し、その[[鎮守社]]として[[豊臣氏|豊臣家]]から篤い崇敬を受けた。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[天正]]4年（[[1576年]]）の戦火で荒廃してしまっていたが、[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]により[[神社建築|社殿]]や高殿（舞台）が再建された[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009691.html 大阪市：59．玉造稲荷神社] 2022年4月10日閲覧。しかし、[[慶長]]20年（[[1615年]]）の[[大坂の陣|大坂夏の陣]]で社殿は再び焼失した。 [[元和 (日本)|元和]]5年（[[1619年]]）、[[江戸幕府]]の[[大坂城代]][[内藤信正]]や[[氏神#氏子|氏子]]らの[[寄付|寄進]]によって再建された。社地は元々は急崖に面していたため、少しでも平坦化するために、[[寛政]]元年（[[1789年]]）、[[東横堀川]]の[[浚渫]]で出た土砂を町人らが運び込む「砂持」が行われた。豊臣時代に引き続いて徳川時代も大坂城の鎮守とされ、'''豊津稲荷社'''と称した。 [[江戸時代]]には[[お蔭参り|伊勢参り]]の出発点とされた。[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）11月の大坂大火（新町焼）で焼失するが、[[1871年]]（[[明治]]4年）に再建される。 [[明治]]時代になると[[府社]]に列せられている。[[1891年]]（明治24年）5月27日、[[文秀女王]]が御成。女王はこの際、要望に応じて「真榊の　わか葉の陰に　立よれば　みやゐすヽしき　夕風ぞ吹く」と即興で詠んだ『花の園生』第5号（[[文明社]]、1891年6月）26・27頁。 現在の「玉造」の社名は鎮座地の地名によるもので、一帯は古代、[[勾玉]]などを作っていた玉作部の居住地であったという伝承がある。現在の社殿は[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）6月1日の第2回[[大阪大空襲]]で焼失した後、[[1954年]]（昭和29年）10月に再建されたものである。[[1986年]]（昭和61年）、創祀二千年を記念して境内に難波・玉造資料館が開館した。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂大神]] * 相殿神 - [[シタテルヒメ|下照姫命]]、[[稚日女尊|稚日女命]]、[[ツクヨミ|月読命]]、[[カグツチ|軻偶突智命]] == 境内 == [[画像:Tamatsukuri-Inari-Jinja Toyotomi Hideyori.jpg|200px|thumb|豊臣秀頼公銅像]] * 本殿 - [[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）10月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1954年（昭和29年）10月再建。 * 白龍池 * [[厳嶋神社]] - 祭神：[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]]。現在地の池（白龍池）に白龍観音が出現したことにより[[弁才天]]を祀ったもので、[[神仏分離]]により祭神を市杵嶋姫神とした。雨乞いの神として信仰される。 * 万慶[[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：宇迦之御魂大神。[[大坂城]]内の各所に祀られていた稲荷社を、[[享保]]年間（[[1716年]] - [[1736年]]）に一社に合祀し当社境内に祀ったもの。 * 新山稲荷神社 - 祭神：宇迦之御魂大神。元は[[寛政]]11年（[[1799年]]）、当時の[[大坂城代]]・[[松平輝和]]が城代屋敷内に当社の分霊を祀ったものである。[[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）、当社境内に遷座された。新山稲荷は現在の[[大阪府立清水谷高等学校]]敷地内に在った。 * 胞衣塚大明神 - 祭神：[[豊臣秀頼]]の[[胞衣]]。当初は大坂城三の丸に当たる現在地に胞衣が埋められ、その後は豊臣家を慕う当地の人々により密かに祀られていた。[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）、戦災により焼失し、寺山町（現・上町1丁目）、東阪町（現・玉造1丁目）に遷座していた。[[1983年]]（昭和58年）、大坂築城400年を記念して元の鎮座地に遷座された。子供の夜泣きに霊験ありとされる。 * 豊臣家奉納鳥居 - [[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）、豊臣秀頼により社殿再建時に奉納された石[[鳥居]]。[[阪神・淡路大震災]]により基礎に損傷を受け、現在は上部・脚部に分けて境内に置かれている。 * 豊臣秀頼公銅像 - [[2011年]]（[[平成]]23年）10月13日に建立・除幕式。 * 利休井 - 当社の南側に屋敷を構えていた[[千利休]]にあやかって[[2006年]]（平成18年）に作ったもの。 * 社務所 * 参集殿 * 難波・玉造資料館 - [[1986年]]（昭和61年）築。 * 分社 ** [[玉造稲荷神社分社]]（大阪府大阪市中央区上町1丁目8-4） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * [[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[玉造 (大阪市)|玉造]]2-3-8 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅： - [[大阪環状線]]または[[Osaka Metro中央線|地下鉄中央線]]・[[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線|長堀鶴見緑地線]][[森ノ宮駅]]下車後、徒歩約6分（南西へ約500m） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ 玉造稲荷神社ホームページ] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまつくりいなりしんしや}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:1954年竣工の日本の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[玉造稲荷神社]] to [[Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 稲荷神社 |画像 = [[画像:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
拝殿 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[玉造 (大阪市)|玉造]]2丁目3-8 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 40 |緯度秒 = 40.6 |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 31 |経度秒 = 47.8 |祭神 = [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂大神]]、[[シタテルヒメ|下照姫命]]、[[稚日女尊|稚日女命]]、[[ツクヨミ|月読命]]、[[カグツチ|軻偶突智命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[府社]] |創建 = 伝・[[垂仁天皇]]18年 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = 玉造稲荷神社（通称）
比売社（旧称）
豊津稲荷社（旧称） |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Osaka city }} '''玉造稲荷神社'''（たまつくりいなりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[玉造 (大阪市)|玉造]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[府社]]。[[法人登記|登記]]上の[[宗教法人]]名称は'''稲荷神社'''（いなりじんじゃ）。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば[[垂仁天皇]]18年（[[紀元前12年]]）に[[天皇]]によって創建され、当時は'''[[比売神|比売社]]'''と称していた。[[蘇我氏]]と[[物部氏]]の戦いの際、蘇我氏方の[[聖徳太子]]がこの地、玉作岡に布陣して戦勝を祈願し、戦勝後当地に[[観音堂]]を建てたという伝承がある。 豊臣[[大坂城]]の三の丸に位置し、その[[鎮守社]]として[[豊臣氏|豊臣家]]から篤い崇敬を受けた。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[天正]]4年（[[1576年]]）の戦火で荒廃してしまっていたが、[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]により[[神社建築|社殿]]や高殿（舞台）が再建された[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kensetsu/page/0000009691.html 大阪市：59．玉造稲荷神社] 2022年4月10日閲覧。しかし、[[慶長]]20年（[[1615年]]）の[[大坂の陣|大坂夏の陣]]で社殿は再び焼失した。 [[元和 (日本)|元和]]5年（[[1619年]]）、[[江戸幕府]]の[[大坂城代]][[内藤信正]]や[[氏神#氏子|氏子]]らの[[寄付|寄進]]によって再建された。社地は元々は急崖に面していたため、少しでも平坦化するために、[[寛政]]元年（[[1789年]]）、[[東横堀川]]の[[浚渫]]で出た土砂を町人らが運び込む「砂持」が行われた。豊臣時代に引き続いて徳川時代も大坂城の鎮守とされ、'''豊津稲荷社'''と称した。 [[江戸時代]]には[[お蔭参り|伊勢参り]]の出発点とされた。[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）11月の大坂大火（新町焼）で焼失するが、[[1871年]]（[[明治]]4年）に再建される。 [[明治]]時代になると[[府社]]に列せられている。[[1891年]]（明治24年）5月27日、[[文秀女王]]が御成。女王はこの際、要望に応じて「真榊の　わか葉の陰に　立よれば　みやゐすヽしき　夕風ぞ吹く」と即興で詠んだ『花の園生』第5号（[[文明社]]、1891年6月）26・27頁。 現在の「玉造」の社名は鎮座地の地名によるもので、一帯は古代、[[勾玉]]などを作っていた玉作部の居住地であったという伝承がある。現在の社殿は[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）6月1日の第2回[[大阪大空襲]]で焼失した後、[[1954年]]（昭和29年）10月に再建されたものである。[[1986年]]（昭和61年）、創祀二千年を記念して境内に難波・玉造資料館が開館した。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂大神]] * 相殿神 - [[シタテルヒメ|下照姫命]]、[[稚日女尊|稚日女命]]、[[ツクヨミ|月読命]]、[[カグツチ|軻偶突智命]] == 境内 == [[画像:Tamatsukuri-Inari-Jinja Toyotomi Hideyori.jpg|200px|thumb|豊臣秀頼公銅像]] * 本殿 - [[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）10月再建。 * 拝殿 - 1954年（昭和29年）10月再建。 * 白龍池 * [[厳嶋神社]] - 祭神：[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]]。現在地の池（白龍池）に白龍観音が出現したことにより[[弁才天]]を祀ったもので、[[神仏分離]]により祭神を市杵嶋姫神とした。雨乞いの神として信仰される。 * 万慶[[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：宇迦之御魂大神。[[大坂城]]内の各所に祀られていた稲荷社を、[[享保]]年間（[[1716年]] - [[1736年]]）に一社に合祀し当社境内に祀ったもの。 * 新山稲荷神社 - 祭神：宇迦之御魂大神。元は[[寛政]]11年（[[1799年]]）、当時の[[大坂城代]]・[[松平輝和]]が城代屋敷内に当社の分霊を祀ったものである。[[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）、当社境内に遷座された。新山稲荷は現在の[[大阪府立清水谷高等学校]]敷地内に在った。 * 胞衣塚大明神 - 祭神：[[豊臣秀頼]]の[[胞衣]]。当初は大坂城三の丸に当たる現在地に胞衣が埋められ、その後は豊臣家を慕う当地の人々により密かに祀られていた。[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）、戦災により焼失し、寺山町（現・上町1丁目）、東阪町（現・玉造1丁目）に遷座していた。[[1983年]]（昭和58年）、大坂築城400年を記念して元の鎮座地に遷座された。子供の夜泣きに霊験ありとされる。 * 豊臣家奉納鳥居 - [[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）、豊臣秀頼により社殿再建時に奉納された石[[鳥居]]。[[阪神・淡路大震災]]により基礎に損傷を受け、現在は上部・脚部に分けて境内に置かれている。 * 豊臣秀頼公銅像 - [[2011年]]（[[平成]]23年）10月13日に建立・除幕式。 * 利休井 - 当社の南側に屋敷を構えていた[[千利休]]にあやかって[[2006年]]（平成18年）に作ったもの。 * 社務所 * 参集殿 * 難波・玉造資料館 - [[1986年]]（昭和61年）築。 * 分社 ** [[玉造稲荷神社分社]]（大阪府大阪市中央区上町1丁目8-4） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * [[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[玉造 (大阪市)|玉造]]2-3-8 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅： - [[大阪環状線]]または[[Osaka Metro中央線|地下鉄中央線]]・[[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線|長堀鶴見緑地線]][[森ノ宮駅]]下車後、徒歩約6分（南西へ約500m） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ 玉造稲荷神社ホームページ] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまつくりいなりしんしや}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:稲荷神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:1954年竣工の日本の建築物]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[Inari (mythology)|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}}

A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]]

Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[Inari (mythology)|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[Inari (mythology)|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]]

The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:c]][[Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]]

Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[Category:Osaka City shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[Inari (mythology)|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:1954年竣工の日本の建築物]]

Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[Inari (mythology)|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:1954年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]]

Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Fr]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}}

It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[Inari (mythology)|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[Inari (mythology)|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]
