{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[Meiji period]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[Osaka Loop Line]] or [[Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}}

The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}}

Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}}

A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}}

Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}}

A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}}

Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}}

Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}}

After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}}

Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari]]. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 1580s to protect [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]].[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], the shrine was located within the third compound of [[:en:Osaka Castle|Osaka Castle]] and served as its [[:en:Chinjusha|guardian shrine]] under the patronage of the [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]]. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]] in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the [[:en:Placenta|Placenta]] of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from [[:en:Morinomiya Station|Morinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Metro|Osaka Metro]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]] subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]
