It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3514709]] Wikidata name: Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine}} | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari}}. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} in the 1580s to protect {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle}}.[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō Ward}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}} during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, the shrine was located within the third compound of {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle}} and served as its {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine}} under the patronage of the {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|WD=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan}}. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|en|Siege of Osaka|WD=Q475780|lt=Siege of Osaka}} in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the {{ill|Placenta|en|Placenta|WD=Q1212935|lt=Placenta}} of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori}} in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from {{ill|Morinomiya Station|en|Morinomiya Station|WD=Q1131146|lt=Morinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Metro|en|Osaka Metro|WD=Q51211653|lt=Osaka Metro}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}} subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}}. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} in the 1580s to protect {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle|1=Osaka Castle}}.[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, the shrine was located within the third compound of {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle|1=Osaka Castle}} and served as its {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} under the patronage of the {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|WD=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan|1=Toyotomi clan}}. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|en|Siege of Osaka|WD=Q475780|lt=Siege of Osaka|1=Siege of Osaka}} in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the {{ill|Placenta|en|Placenta|WD=Q1212935|lt=Placenta|1=Placenta}} of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from {{ill|Morinomiya Station|en|Morinomiya Station|WD=Q1131146|lt=Morinomiya Station|1=Morinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line|1=Osaka Loop Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Metro|en|Osaka Metro|WD=Q51211653|lt=Osaka Metro|1=Osaka Metro (company)}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}} subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the {{ill|Placenta|en|Placenta|WD=Q1212935|lt=Placenta|1=Placenta}} of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from {{ill|Morinomiya Station|en|Morinomiya Station|WD=Q1131146|lt=Morinomiya Station|1=Morinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line|1=Osaka Loop Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Metro|en|Osaka Metro|WD=Q51211653|lt=Osaka Metro|1=Osaka Metro (company)}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}} subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}}. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} in the 1580s to protect {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle|1=Osaka Castle}}.[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, the shrine was located within the third compound of {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle|1=Osaka Castle}} and served as its {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} under the patronage of the {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|WD=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan|1=Toyotomi clan}}. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|en|Siege of Osaka|WD=Q475780|lt=Siege of Osaka|1=Siege of Osaka}} in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the {{ill|Placenta|en|Placenta|WD=Q1212935|lt=Placenta|1=Placenta}} of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from {{ill|Morinomiya Station|en|Morinomiya Station|WD=Q1131146|lt=Morinomiya Station|1=Morinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line|1=Osaka Loop Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Metro|en|Osaka Metro|WD=Q51211653|lt=Osaka Metro|1=Osaka Metro (company)}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}} subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the {{ill|Placenta|en|Placenta|WD=Q1212935|lt=Placenta|1=Placenta}} of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from {{ill|Morinomiya Station|en|Morinomiya Station|WD=Q1131146|lt=Morinomiya Station|1=Morinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line|1=Osaka Loop Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Metro|en|Osaka Metro|WD=Q51211653|lt=Osaka Metro|1=Osaka Metro (company)}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}} subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja]][[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]][[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]][[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}} | image = Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|41|N|135|31|48|E|region:JP_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine | image = [[File:Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja haiden.jpeg|250px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 12 | mapframe-width = 250 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|40.6|N|135|31|47.8|E|type:landmark|display=inline}} | location = 2-3-8 Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture | deity = [[Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] | established = Traditionally said to be founded in {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}'s 18th year | architecture= Reconstructed in 1954 | type = {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} | cultural = Former Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]) }} '''{{nihongo|Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine|玉造稲荷神社|Tamatsukuri-Inari-jinja}}''' is a shrine dedicated to the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' ('god') {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}}. Its construction can be traced to 12 BCE, and Inari was enshrined there by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} in the 1580s to protect {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle|1=Osaka Castle}}.[http://www.tourism.city.osaka.jp/en/search_osaka/reference/sights.php Osaka Visitor's Guide] '''Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine''' (玉造稲荷神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], Japan. Its official registered name as a religious corporation is simply '''Inari Shrine''' (稲荷神社). The shrine enshrines several deities, including [[Ukanomitama]], and has a long history connected to the region and historical figures like {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established during the 18th year of Emperor Suinin's reign (circa 12 BCE). Initially known as the '''Hime Shrine''' (比売社), it is said to have been a place of prayer for victory by {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} during the battle between the [[Soga clan]] and [[Mononobe clan]]. After achieving victory, Prince Shōtoku is believed to have constructed a [[Kannon]] Hall at this site. During the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, the shrine was located within the third compound of {{ill|Osaka Castle|en|Osaka Castle|WD=Q321242|lt=Osaka Castle|1=Osaka Castle}} and served as its {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=guardian shrine|1=Chinjusha}} under the patronage of the {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|WD=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan|1=Toyotomi clan}}. The original shrine buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|en|Siege of Osaka|WD=Q475780|lt=Siege of Osaka|1=Siege of Osaka}} in 1615. The shrine was reconstructed in 1619 through donations by Osaka's [[castle warden]], [[Naitō Nobumasa]], and local residents. Subsequent renovations occurred during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, including land reclamation efforts in 1789. The shrine played a significant role as the departure point for pilgrims traveling to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]. It was damaged again by fire in 1863 and rebuilt in 1871. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine ([[府社]]), and the current name, Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine, reflects its location. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[1945 Osaka air raids]] and reconstructed in 1954. A museum, the Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive, was established in 1986 to commemorate the shrine's 2,000th anniversary. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Ukanomitama]] – deity of agriculture and fertility. * [[Shitateruhime]], [[Wakahime]], [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto]], [[Kagutsuchi-no-Mikoto]] – Additional associated deities. == Grounds and Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in 1954 after war destruction. * **White Dragon Pond** – Associated with a [[Benzaiten]] deity. * **Statue of Toyotomi Hideyori** – Erected in 2011. * **Hoanden Shrine** – Enshrines the {{ill|Placenta|en|Placenta|WD=Q1212935|lt=Placenta|1=Placenta}} of Toyotomi Hideyori. * **Stone Torii Gate** – Donated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} in 1603. * **Nanba-Tamatsukuri Historical Archive** – Opened in 1986. == Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot southwest from {{ill|Morinomiya Station|en|Morinomiya Station|WD=Q1131146|lt=Morinomiya Station|1=Morinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line|1=Osaka Loop Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Metro|en|Osaka Metro|WD=Q51211653|lt=Osaka Metro|1=Osaka Metro (company)}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.inari.or.jp/ Official website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==location== The shrine is a short walk north from exit #1 of the Tamatsukuri Station on the Nagahori-tsurumi-ryokuchi Line of the {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}} subway system. It is close to the [[:en:Japan Railways|JR]] Loop Line. The address is 2-3-8, Tamatsukuri, Chūō-ku, Osaka City, 540-0004. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} Website (Japanese): [http://www.inari.or.jp www.inari.or.jp] ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Inari Faith}} {{translated page|ja|玉造稲荷神社|version=103869960|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Tamatsukuri Inari Shrine]] [[fr:Tamatsukuri Inari-jinja]] [[ja:玉造稲荷神社]] {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{Commons category|Tamatsukuri-inari-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q3514709}} [[Category:12 BC establishments]] [[Category:1954 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 1954]] [[Category:Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
